data.title: Logitech Non-GAAP EPS of $1.20, revenue of $1.12B, raises guidance
data.description:
• Logitech press release (NASDAQ:LOGI): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.20.
  • Revenue of $1.12B (+5.7% Y/Y).
  • GAAP gross margin was 43.6 percent, up 210 basis points compared to Q2 of the prior year.

  • Logitech raised its full-year outlook for Fiscal Year 2025:

    Previous FY25 outlook

    New FY25 outlook

    Sales

    $4.34 - $4.43 billion

    $4.39 - $4.47 billion

    Sales growth (in US dollars, year over year)

    1% - 3%

    2% - 4%

    Non-GAAP operating income

    $700 - $730 million

    $720 - $750 million

    Non-GAAP op. inc. growth (year over year)

    0% - 4%

    3% - 7%

data.title: Tuesday's Economic Calendar
data.description:
10:00 AM Fed’s Harker: “Fintech”
Philadelphia Federal Reserve Bank President Patrick Harker to speak on "Fintech" before the Eighth Annual Fintech Conference hosted by the Federal Reserve Bank of Philadelphia.
10:00 AM Richmond Fed Mfg. Index
The Richmond Manufacturing Index rates the relative level of business conditions among manufacturers including shipments, new orders, and employment. The October Richmond Manufacturing Index is expected to increase modestly to -15 after reporting sluggish activity in September. The composite manufacturing index edged down from −19 in August to −21 in September.
11:00 AM Treasury Buyback Announcement (Preliminary)
The Treasury conducts buybacks to improve liquidity for certain maturities, limit variations in auction sizes, and reduce maturity peaks in outstanding debt.
1:00 PM Money Supply
The money supply is the total amount of money—cash, coins, and balances in bank accounts—in circulation.
Click here to see the weekly economic calendar.

data.title: Mangoceuticals refutes claims made by Lilly in weight-loss drug lawsuit
data.description:
Mangoceuticals (NASDAQ:MGRX), which operates the men’s health platform MangoRx, said it strongly refutes any and all claims made in a lawsuit filed against the company by Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) over a compounded version of Lilly’s popular weight-loss drug tirzepatide.

Lilly has accused MangoRx (MGRX) of improperly marketing an oral formulation of tirzepatide. Lilly (LLY) markets injected formulations of tirzepatide under the brand names Mounjaro and Zepbound.

“MangoRx (MGRX) believes it has strong arguments against Eli Lilly’s claims and intends to vigorously defend itself in this matter,” Mangoceuticals said in a statement.

Earlier Monday, Reuters reported that Lilly had filed lawsuits against three companies over the marketing of compounded versions of tirzepatide. The three companies are Pivotal Peptides, MangoRx (MGRX) and Genesis Lifestyle Medicine.

Mangoceuticals (MGRX) stock rallied in May after the company announced it planned to sell proprietary oral versions of Lilly's (LLY) tirzepatide and Novo Nordisk's (NVO) semaglutide, which is sold under the brand names Ozempic and Wegovy.

Mangoceuticals (MGRX) held its initial public offering in March 2023.

data.title: Oil bounces as China cuts interest rates; Saudi Aramco CEO 'fairly bullish' on China
data.description:
Crude oil futures jumped Monday, recovering a slice of last week's sharp decline, after China lowered its benchmark lending rates as part of a broader package of stimulus measures aimed at reviving its economy, sending a positive signal to markets, and Israel continued its operations against Hamas and Hezbollah targets in the Middle East.

The People's Bank of China cut rates "more than expected as part of a series of economic stimulus measures that should support demand prospects for crude," XS.com senior strategist Samer Hasn said.

But China's moves have been met with skepticism over their effectiveness in supporting the economy, so he believes oil's gains, supported by the economic factors from China, "may be fragile and subject to rapid reversal."

Gains also were supported as Saudi Aramco (ARMCO) CEO Amin Nasser reportedly told an energy conference that he is "fairly bullish" on Chinese oil demand in light of the stepped-up policy support aimed at boosting growth.

"We see more demand for jet fuel and naphtha especially for liquid-to-chemical projects," Nasser said on the sidelines of the Singapore International Energy Week conference.

The CEO also reiterated Aramco's (ARMCO) aim to increase its liquids-to-chemical capacity to 4M bbl/day, with a focus on China.

Front-month Nymex crude (CL1:COM) for November delivery settled +1.9% to $70.56/bbl, and front-month December Brent (CO1:COM) ended +1.7% to $74.29/bbl.

Meanwhile, U.S. natural gas (NG1:COM) clawed back a bit of last week's losses, with the front-month November contract closing +2.4% to $2.312/MMBtu.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

However, the head of the International Energy Agency said Monday that China's oil-demand growth is expected to remain weak in 2025 despite the stimulus measures, as the country electrifies its car fleet and grows at a slower pace.

Last week, China's government reported its economy grew by 4.6% in Q3, the slowest pace in 18 months, and its refinery output fell for the sixth straight month, adding to concerns over sluggish oil demand.

data.title: Top BBVA sells stake over opposition to hostile bid for Sabadell: FT
Saudi Arabia's United Electronics plans IPO for finance unit
data.description:
• United Electronics, a Saudi Arabian company known as Extra, is moving ahead with the listing of a 30% interest in its financial services unit on the country’s main market.
  • The unit is called United International Holding and is the parent of Tasheel Finance, which was established in 2019 to provide financing for customers. The plan is to sell 7.5 million shares with the proceeds going to Extra, according to an announcement.
  • Financial-services firms HSBC Saudi Arabia and EFG Hermes KSA were hired to handle the initial public offering.
  • Tasheel generated 59 million riyals ($15.7 million) in profit in the third quarter, 11% more than a year earlier.

data.title: LVMH's Arnault family, Red Bull in talks to buy majority stake in French club Paris FC
data.description:
French billionaire and LVMH (OTCPK:LVMHF) chairman Bernard Arnault has partnered with Austrian energy drinks company Red Bull to buy a majority stake in iconic soccer club Paris FC.

Agache, the holding company of the Arnault family, and Red Bull have entered into exclusive negotiations to acquire a majority stake in Paris FC, the club said in a statement.

The club did not disclose any financial details of the deal.

According to the statement, Agache plans to bring to the club its "entrepreneurial vision and all her know-how in terms of economic development", while Red Bull will "mainly intervene in the sporting dimension" in an advisory role.

Paris FC stated that Agache intends to establish both the men’s and women’s side among the elite of French football. The company will work with Paris FC president Pierre Ferracci to achieve its goals.

Earlier in the month, LVMH (OTCPK:LVMHF) entered into a 10-year partnership with Liberty Media (LLYVA)-owned Formula One. The luxury giant also invested about $160 million to sponsor the Paris 2024 Olympic and Paralympic Summer Games.

Antoine Arnault, the son of Bernard Arnault, will represent the family on the Paris FC board.

"With our support, as well as the precious support of our partner Red Bull, we are hopeful that, gradually, we will write a beautiful page of French football together," he said in a statement.

Paris Football Club, commonly referred to as Paris FC, was founded in 1969 before merging with Stade Saint-Germain to form Paris Saint-Germain in 1970. In 1972, the club broke away from Paris Saint-Germain, resulting in the current Paris FC.

The professional soccer club competes in and sits at the top of Ligue 2, the second tier of French football. It hasn't played in France’s top league since the late 1970s.

data.title: Shell pipeline in Singapore leaked oil, local authorities say
data.description:
An oil leakage from a pipeline in Singapore operated by Shell (NYSE:SHEL) was reported early Sunday, according to local officials. They said a cleanup was under way.

The leak was reported to have “stopped at source,” the Maritime and Port Authority of Singapore said in a statement. The leak occurred between Bukom Island and Bukom Kecil at 5:30 a.m. local time, it said.

“Shell (SHEL) has placed containment booms off the site of the leak and has also deployed craft equipped with dispersants to clean up the oil sighted in the vicinity of the leak,” the authority said. “There are no new oil sightings” as of 6 p.m.

data.title: S&P 500 to post 3% annualized return over next 10 years, Goldman says
11 large-cap stocks whose dividend yield is more than 4%
data.description:
U.S. stocks have climbed to record highs with the Standard & Poor’s 500 stock index (SP500) gaining 23% for the year through Friday. Meanwhile, the dividend yield for the SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (NYSEARCA:SPY) currently stands at about 1.2%.

That said, dividend-seeking investors can still find large-cap stocks that offer a yield of more than 4%, as the following Seeking Alpha screener shows. The list includes companies outside the traditional dividend payers in the utilities, pipelines and real estate investment trust categories.
